MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-23-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Roger D. Yuen of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / moving party Flowserve US Inc. as sucessor-in-interest to Durametallic Corporation
Patrick R. Campbell of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Billy Colton

RULING:
DEFENDANT FLOWSERVE US, INC. AS SUCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO DURAMETALLIC CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

The parties stipulated to apply the same argument of Flowserve US Inc.'s motion for summary judgment in the Schmeltz case (# 276174). The Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant Flowserve US, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of submitting evidence to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claim. Plaintiff's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826 as to product identification. As to asbestos content of the Durametallic packing, plaintiff's deposition testimony and Mr. Smith's declaration, the only evidence defendant relied upon on this topic, are insufficient to sustain defendant's burden. Plaintiff's objections to Mr. Smith's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Early K. Langley of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #3537 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)
